name: Grímsvötn
eruptionHistory: Iceland's most frequently erupting volcano. Major eruptions in 1996, 1998, 2004, and 2011. The 2011 ash cloud caused flight cancellations.
description: Grímsvötn is Iceland's most active volcano, located beneath the Vatnajökull ice cap. Its eruptions produce glacial floods and ash clouds that can disrupt aviation.
